source.title: Accident Investigation Report - West Delta 31 Platform E-DRL Allision

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - West Delta Block 31

actions_taken: Production operator transported to onshore medical facility
M&H engineering commissioned to survey damage
Structure model analysis performed with 15 support members removed
Safe Boarding Plan submitted to BSEE
Platform operating under approved Safe Operations Plan with 20 ft wave height restriction
event.summary: On 19 November 2022 at approximately 0430 hours, motor vessel Jacob Gerald allided into West Delta 31 Platform E-DRL while transporting personnel. The captain dozed off before impact. The vessel was traveling at cruising speed of 8 knots when the captain fell asleep. A production operator aboard the vessel reported back soreness and was transported to an onshore medical facility, later released to full duty (first aid case). The allision caused damage to several structural members. The platform was shut in and the damaged area barricaded. Engineering analysis determined the platform was safe to man with wave heights below 20 feet. Total repair costs remain unknown but not expected to exceed $25K.
key_phrases: safe to man as long as the maximum wave height was below 20 feet
Human Performance Error - Inattention to task/Fatigue

threats.name: Loss of Vessel Control Due to Captain Fatigue

controls.name: Captain Alertness and Attention to Navigation
controls.lod_basis: Direct operational control by vessel captain

supporting_text: the captain dozed off sometime before impact
Captain fell asleep at the helm
Human Performance Error - Inattention to task/Fatigue

controls.name: Vessel Speed Management During Transit
controls.barrier_role: Control vessel approach speed to platform
controls.lod_basis: Operational procedure for safe vessel transit

supporting_text: The trip was to take about 30 minutes at the M/V's cruising speed of 8 knots
AIS data shows the vessel going from 8.6 knots to 1.7 knots at 0434 hours

controls.name: Automatic Identification System (AIS)
controls.barrier_role: Monitor and track vessel position and speed
controls.lod_basis: Independent monitoring system for vessel tracking

supporting_text: Automatic Identification System (AIS) data obtained by the USCG showing the vessel's transit and speed was shared with BSEE
The AIS data shows the vessel going from 8.6 knots to 1.7 knots at 0434 hours

controls.name: Platform Structural Design and Integrity
controls.barrier_role: Withstand impact and maintain structural integrity
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to prevent structural collapse

supporting_text: causing damage to several structural members
M&H performed a structure model analysis of the structure with 15 support members removed
It was determined by M&H that the platform was safe to man as long as the maximum wave height was below 20 feet

controls.name: Emergency Response and Platform Shutdown
controls.lod_basis: Secondary response to secure facility after incident

supporting_text: The platform was shut in and the area of the production deck, above where the damage occurred from the allision, was barricaded off to prevent anyone from entering
BSEE requested Cox shut in the facility until a professional engineer could evaluate the damage

controls.name: Medical Evaluation and Treatment
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident recovery action for injured personnel

supporting_text: A production operator (PO) aboard the M/V at the time of the incident reported having soreness in his back and was taken to an onshore medical facility for further evaluation
The PO was released back to full duty without restrictions and the injury was classified as a first aid case

controls.name: Engineering Assessment and Safe Boarding Plan
controls.barrier_role: Verify structural safety and establish safe operating limits
controls.lod_basis: Independent verification of structural integrity post-incident

supporting_text: Cox commissioned M&H engineering and consulting firm to survey the damage
M&H performed a structure model analysis of the structure with 15 support members removed and various combinations of main deck live load and wave height applied
Cox submitted a Safe Boarding plan and the third party engineering survey to the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) on 8 December 2022
On 19 November 2022 at approximately 1000 hours, BSEE received notification from Cox that an allision occurred
On 22 November 2022, BSEE accompanied the United States Coast Guard (USCG) arriving at WD 31 E and conducted an onsite investigation
BSEE concurred with the Safe Boarding Plan and Engineering Survey

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
